w:r.w:t: 2678 Holdings LLC — Internal / Claude (primary build resource)

This Database Specification (DBSPEC) is the physical database design authority for the TCA Onboarding Engine. It translates the logical data model specified in TCA-ONBENG-TRD-2026-001 Final v1.0 and the architectural decisions recorded in its predecessor (TCA-ONBENG-DATAARCH-2026-DRAFT-001) into the complete, DDL-ready physical schema against which the Senior Systems Engineer provisions Aurora PostgreSQL and the Claude Code application build executes migrations.
The DBSPEC occupies a distinct position in the document hierarchy:
Anchor memorandum. Cross-engine governance, CRO authority, retention standardization. Governs where this DBSPEC is inconsistent with the TRD.

Requirements authority. Functional and non-functional requirements; integration contracts (incl. event envelope and inbound/outbound payload field specs); workflow state machine; separation of duties; RIA-readiness; API governance. Governs application behaviour and data obligations.
Physical database authority. DDL, index strategy, constraint rules, extension configuration, instance topology, migration policy. Governs SE provisioning and Claude Code schema implementation.

Companion Reference Engine DBSPEC. The Onboarding ↔ Reference contract is implemented on both sides; this DBSPEC is read alongside its counterpart for any change touching the event envelope, payload fields, or controlled-access token model.
Where any inconsistency exists between this DBSPEC and the Anchor Memorandum, the Anchor Memorandum governs. Where any inconsistency exists between this DBSPEC and the Governing TRD, the TRD governs.

This document is build-governing. No schema migration may be applied to the production Aurora cluster that is not traceable to a requirement in this DBSPEC or to a documented amendment approved by MRB and Stan Hua.

Platform: Amazon Web Services (AWS). Resolved in TCA-ONBENG-TRD-2026-001 Final v1.0 §21 and §26 (Cloud platform — RESOLVED). This decision is closed. All database infrastructure SHALL be provisioned on AWS managed services. The existing VPS is staging/transitional only and SHALL NOT carry production data.
uuid-ossp; pg_trgm (fuzzy name match for parties); btree_gin (jsonb composite indexes). pgvector and PostGIS are NOT enabled on this cluster.

1 primary writer + 1 read replica. Multi-AZ deployment. (Reader added at scale milestone; MVP traffic is comfortably writer-only, but the replica is provisioned from day one for failover and reporting workloads.)
db.r6g.large or equivalent (2 vCPU / 16 GB RAM). Resize post-MVP based on measured load.
db.r6g.large or equivalent (2 vCPU / 16 GB RAM).
Aurora auto-scaling storage. Starting allocation: 50 GB. Scale automatically.
AWS KMS customer-managed key (CMK). Key rotation: annual minimum.
Automated continuous backup. Point-in-time recovery window: 35 days. Daily snapshots retained 30 days. Long-term snapshots per retention policy (§11).
Automated Multi-AZ failover. Target failover time: < 30 seconds.
Amazon CloudWatch. Custom metrics: Aurora replica lag, active connections, query latency p95/p99, storage usage, integration_event backlog depth.
NOT part of production deployment. Staging/transitional only per Anchor Memo §13.
Application services connect to Aurora via the following endpoints:
State mutations only. Reads served from reader where latency permits.
Executes state transitions and gating-rule evaluation.
Receives inbound events from Reference and Risk; validates HMAC signature; routes to handlers.
Writes only. Append-only audit event insertion. No UPDATE/DELETE grants.
Creates upload sessions, file hashes, S3 object metadata, controlled-access tokens, classification tasks.
Syncs Google Workspace identity provider users into user_account_shadow.
Staff-side interface backend. Authenticated via OAuth bound to role:analyst, role:onboarding_admin, role:principal, role:cro.
External client upload portal. Scoped to role:client_upload_user permissions only.
Migration runner connects to writer. Applied via CI/CD pipeline only.

Aurora is deployed in a private subnet. No public internet access. Application services connect via VPC private networking. S3 access uses VPC Gateway Endpoints. ECS Fargate and Lambda services are deployed in private subnets with NAT Gateway egress for external API calls (Google Workspace SCIM, QuickBooks API, webhook destinations to Reference and Risk endpoints).
All database credentials are stored exclusively in AWS Secrets Manager. No credential may be stored in source code, environment files, container images, or any engine database table.
The following PostgreSQL extensions SHALL be enabled on the Aurora cluster before any schema migration is applied:
UUID generation function uuid_generate_v4(). Required for default primary key values.
Trigram indexes for fuzzy text matching on firm legal_name, normalized_name, and person name.
Composite GIN indexes on jsonb columns (authorized_scope, requested_artifacts, payload, permission_set, etc.).
Hashing helpers for file_hash verification and payload_hash computation.
Enable extensions with the following DDL (applied once per cluster, not per schema):
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS btree_gin;
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto;

Extension versions should be pinned in infrastructure-as-code (AWS CDK or Terraform). Aurora managed updates may change available versions; test version upgrades in staging before applying to production. Note that this DBSPEC does not enable pgvector or PostGIS — they are enabled on the Reference Engine cluster, not the Onboarding cluster.

The Onboarding Engine database is organised into ten logical schema groups, each corresponding to a distinct functional domain. Each schema is a PostgreSQL schema (namespace) within the Aurora cluster database. Schema names align with TCA-ONBENG-TRD-2026-001 Final v1.0 §9.
Internal firm, sponsor, counterparty, person, contact method, address, identifiers, party relationships.
Identity provider mirrors, roles, project/engagement-scoped access grants, revocation, access review.
Google Workspace is the authoritative IdP.
Engagement records, service-package metadata, fee model, billing profile, legal/accounting package status.
Project/opportunity records created under an approved engagement.
Document request, upload session, uploaded document, classification, retention binding, controlled-access tokens.
Issues short-lived access tokens to Reference Engine.
State machine templates, instances, transitions, checklist items, task assignments.
Master orchestration state machine per TRD §12.
RIA-readiness, onboarding exceptions, compliance reviews, conflict checks, policy acknowledgments, approval records.
Includes 15-category gating exception catalog per TRD §13.2.
Outbound event delivery, inbound event reception, idempotency keys, webhook signing key registry, information requests.
Bidirectional event contract with Reference and Risk.
Local audit mirror and shared audit store integration. Hash-chained. Immutable.
Shared append-only audit store (all engines write)
Reusable overlay configuration for TCA now and TCM/TCN later.
TCA overlay active; TCM and TCN placeholders.
All tables in all schemas SHALL include the following columns unless explicitly noted otherwise:
Set by the application at insert time. Never updated.
Updated by application or trigger on every UPDATE. Not present on append-only tables.
Identity of the producing service or user. Format: service:{svc_name} or user:{user_id}.
Foreign reference to the shared audit store event that recorded this write. Nullable only where the write is itself an audit store insertion.

The following PostgreSQL enum types SHALL be created before any table DDL is applied. All enum types are created in the public schema and referenced by qualified name across all engine schemas. Enum values align with TCA-ONBENG-TRD-2026-001 Final v1.0 Appendix A.

The following sections specify the complete physical DDL for each schema group. Column definitions follow the format: column_name TYPE constraints. All tables include the universal columns defined in §4.3 unless noted. Index definitions follow immediately after each table DDL. For brevity and readability, low-volume reference tables (e.g., enum-driven lookup tables, overlay configuration tables) are described by purpose rather than full DDL — those follow the same column conventions and are seeded at deployment from infrastructure-as-code.

Internal firm, sponsor, counterparty, person, contact, address, identifier, and party-role records. The party schema is the system of record for all internal client/sponsor/operator identity baseline data. It does NOT replicate any data from Reference or Risk.
firm_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
person_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
party_role_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
party_id UUID NOT NULL, -- firm_id or person_id
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
CREATE INDEX idx_pr_party ON party.party_role (party_type, party_id);
beneficial_owner_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
firm_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES party.firm(firm_id),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
Additional tables in party (full DDL deferred to detailed design, follows same column conventions): party_identifier, contact_method, address.

Local mirrors of Google Workspace users, role bindings, permission sets, project/engagement-scoped access grants, access reviews, and access revocations. The user_access_grant table is the system of record for project- and engagement-scoped permissions consumed by Reference and Risk via user_access_grant events.
user_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
identity_provider_id TEXT NOT NULL, -- Google Workspace user id
last_synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
binding_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
granted_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
CREATE INDEX idx_rb_user ON identity_access.role_binding (user_id) WHERE revoked_at IS NULL;
access_grant_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
granted_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
WHERE expires_at IS NOT NULL AND revoked_at IS NULL;
Additional tables in identity_access (column conventions per §4.3): permission_set, access_review, access_revocation.

Commercial/legal engagement and service-package records. The engagement table holds the canonical engagement lifecycle including retention_lock_until computed at closeout per TCA-ONBENG-TRD-2026-001 §15.2.
service_package_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
-- Seeded at deployment with the MVP catalog per TRD §6.1.
The canonical engagement record. retention_lock_until is computed at engagement.closed as closed_at + retention period per §11 of this DBSPEC.
engagement_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
firm_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES party.firm(firm_id),
retention_lock_until TIMESTAMPTZ, -- closed_at + 5 yrs default; 7 yrs tax-related
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
CHECK (status != 'approved' OR approved_by IS NOT NULL),
CHECK (closed_at IS NULL OR retention_lock_until IS NOT NULL)
history_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
engagement_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES engagement.engagement(engagement_id),
performed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
-- Append-only. No UPDATE or DELETE permitted.
Additional tables in engagement (column conventions per §4.3): fee_model, billing_profile, legal_package_status, accounting_package_status.

Project/opportunity records created under an approved engagement. The project record carries the active vertical framework identifier (active_vertical_framework_id) which Reference Engine REQUIRES on the project.created and reference_job.requested events. reference_pack_id and methodology_version_pin are populated when reference_pack.approved is received.
project_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
engagement_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES engagement.engagement(engagement_id),
active_vertical_framework_id TEXT NOT NULL, -- 'TCA-EP-001-v1.0' for MVP
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
CHECK ((reference_pack_id IS NULL AND methodology_version_pin IS NULL)
OR (reference_pack_id IS NOT NULL AND methodology_version_pin IS NOT NULL))
project_role_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES project.project(project_id),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
CREATE INDEX idx_ppr_party ON project.project_party_role (party_type, party_id);
Additional tables in project (column conventions per §4.3): project_scope, project_stage_context, project_material_manifest, project_framework_selection.

Document request lifecycle, upload sessions, uploaded document metadata, classification, retention bindings, and controlled-access tokens. Files themselves live in S3 (see §10); the database stores only metadata, hashes, object URIs, and access-control records.
document_request_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
required BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
information_request_id UUID, -- if originated from Reference IR
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
upload_session_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),

Uploaded documents are the controlled-access object referenced by the document.submitted event consumed by Reference Engine. The controlled_access_token field carries only a vault reference (the token material itself lives in AWS Secrets Manager); controlled_access_expires_at is required in the outbound payload.
document_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
controlled_access_token_ref TEXT, -- vault ARN, not token material
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
CHECK ((controlled_access_token_ref IS NULL AND controlled_access_expires_at IS NULL)
OR (controlled_access_token_ref IS NOT NULL AND controlled_access_expires_at IS NOT NULL))
CREATE INDEX idx_ud_project ON document.uploaded_document (project_id) WHERE project_id IS NOT NULL;
access_rule_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
rule_predicate JSONB NOT NULL, -- role_code / scope_type / scope_id matchers
effective_from TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
Additional tables in document (column conventions per §4.3): document_classification, document_retention_binding.

State machine templates, instances, transitions, checklist items, and task assignments. The workflow.workflow_instance and workflow.workflow_transition tables together implement the orchestration state machine specified in TCA-ONBENG-TRD-2026-001 §12. Permitted transitions are validated in application logic at svc:workflow-engine; the database enforces append-only history.
workflow_template_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
state_set JSONB NOT NULL, -- enumerated states
transition_rules JSONB NOT NULL, -- permitted from→to with guards
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
workflow_instance_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
opened_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
blocked_flag BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
CREATE INDEX idx_wi_scope ON workflow.workflow_instance (scope_type, scope_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_wi_blocked ON workflow.workflow_instance (blocked_flag) WHERE blocked_flag = true;
transition_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
triggered_by TEXT NOT NULL, -- 'user:<uuid>' or 'service:<name>' or 'event:<event_type>'
trigger_event_id UUID, -- inbound event reference if applicable
performed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
-- Append-only. No UPDATE or DELETE permitted.
CREATE INDEX idx_wtr_performed_at ON workflow.workflow_transition (performed_at DESC);
Additional tables in workflow (column conventions per §4.3): workflow_step_template, workflow_step_instance, checklist_item.
RIA-readiness and onboarding review controls including the gating-exception catalog seeded from TCA-ONBENG-TRD-2026-001 §13.2.
Seed table holding the 15-category gating-exception catalog (per TRD §13.2). Populated at deployment from infrastructure-as-code.
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
Active onboarding exception records. source is extended to include reference_engine, risk_engine, and cro per TRD §10. Reference-originated gating exceptions carry information_request_id.
exception_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
raised_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
CREATE INDEX idx_oe_scope ON compliance.onboarding_exception (scope_type, scope_id);
compliance_review_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
engagement_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES engagement.engagement(engagement_id),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
acknowledgment_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
Additional tables in compliance (column conventions per §4.3): conflict_check, advisory_status_flag, approval_record.
Outbound event delivery, inbound event reception, idempotency keys, webhook signing key registry, and information requests received from Reference and Risk.

Outbound event polling source for svc:event-publisher. All envelope fields (event_id, event_type, event_version, occurred_at, producer, correlation_id, idempotency_key) are top-level columns per TRD §11.1 to support idempotency enforcement and event replay.
integration_event_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
event_id UUID NOT NULL, -- envelope event_id
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
CREATE INDEX idx_ie_correlation ON integration.integration_event (correlation_id) WHERE correlation_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_ie_created_at ON integration.integration_event (created_at DESC);

Inbound events received from Reference and Risk. Signature is validated by svc:event-receiver BEFORE the row is committed as 'VALIDATED'. Events with invalid signatures are stored with status 'SIGNATURE_INVALID' and alerted.
inbound_event_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
event_id UUID NOT NULL, -- envelope event_id from producer
received_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
CREATE INDEX idx_in_received_at ON integration.inbound_event (received_at DESC);

Records of information_request.raised events received from Reference (or Risk). When gating_flag=true and severity is CRITICAL or MAJOR, svc:event-receiver automatically creates a corresponding compliance.onboarding_exception with source=reference_engine (or risk_engine) and information_request_id linking to this row.
request_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES project.project(project_id),
engagement_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES engagement.engagement(engagement_id),
source_engine source_engine_enum NOT NULL, -- REFERENCE | RISK
gating_flag BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
CREATE INDEX idx_irr_gating ON integration.information_request_record (gating_flag) WHERE gating_flag = true;
CREATE INDEX idx_irr_correlation ON integration.information_request_record (correlation_id) WHERE correlation_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_irr_artifacts ON integration.information_request_record USING gin (requested_artifacts);
delivery_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
attempted_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
key_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
secrets_manager_arn TEXT NOT NULL, -- AWS Secrets Manager ARN
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
-- Key material lives only in AWS Secrets Manager. This table stores ARN references.

The audit schema mirrors the shared append-only audit store locally. Records are immutable. No DELETE or UPDATE is permitted on any table in this schema. Corrections create new records flagged as corrections with a reference to the corrected event. The hash chain is verifiable by an external reviewer.

The audit schema represents the Onboarding Engine's local mirror of the shared three-engine audit store. The authoritative store is the cross-engine shared instance governed by the Anchor Memorandum. These are complementary — the local mirror provides low-latency access for within-engine queries; the shared store provides cross-engine CRO visibility.
audit_event_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
-- No UPDATE or DELETE ever. Enforced by application logic AND IAM/DB role policy.
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_ael_sequence ON audit.audit_event_local (event_sequence);
CREATE INDEX idx_ael_object ON audit.audit_event_local (object_type, object_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_ael_occurred ON audit.audit_event_local (occurred_at DESC);
signature_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
audit_event_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES audit.audit_event_local(audit_event_id),
signed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
retention_lock_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
legal_hold_flag BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
CHECK (NOT legal_hold_flag OR released_at IS NULL)
CREATE INDEX idx_rl_scope ON audit.retention_lock (scope_type, scope_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_rl_legal_hold ON audit.retention_lock (legal_hold_flag) WHERE legal_hold_flag = true;
Additional tables in audit (column conventions per §4.3): retention_policy, legal_hold, data_export_record.
Reusable overlay configuration for TCA, TCM, and TCN. The TCA overlay is active at MVP; TCM and TCN are placeholders with no live rows until those entity programs are activated.
overlay_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
active_flag BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
CREATE INDEX idx_eo_active ON overlay.entity_overlay (entity_code) WHERE active_flag = true;
Additional tables in overlay (column conventions per §4.3): onboarding_requirement, overlay_workflow_map, overlay_document_requirement, overlay_event_rule.

Unlike the Reference Engine, the Onboarding Engine does not run a high-cardinality retrieval-query workload at MVP and therefore does not require time-range partitioning of its primary tables at first deployment. The only candidates for partitioning are the audit and integration tables once volume crosses operational thresholds.
Unpartitioned at MVP. Reassess at 5 million rows or > 1 year of operational data. Future partition by RANGE(occurred_at) monthly.
Unpartitioned at MVP. Reassess if backlog growth or operational scans degrade. Future partition by RANGE(created_at) monthly with hot/warm separation.
Unpartitioned at MVP. Same reassessment criteria as integration_event.
Unpartitioned at MVP. Append-only table; reassess at 10 million rows.
Unpartitioned at MVP. Low cardinality expected.
Unpartitioned. MVP cardinality does not warrant partition overhead.
Partition introduction is a planned operational change governed by §8. The SE shall include partition-readiness review in the operational readiness milestone at 12 months post-MVP go-live.

All schema changes to the production Aurora cluster must be executed via versioned migration scripts managed by Alembic (or equivalent). Manual DDL changes to the production database are prohibited. The following rules apply:
Alembic with version-controlled migration scripts stored in the application repository.
Every migration script requires code review and approval before execution. Production migrations require MRB and Stan Hua sign-off.
Migrations are applied only via the CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions or AWS CodePipeline). No direct psql execution in production.

Schema changes shall be designed for backward compatibility where possible. Breaking changes require a coordinated deployment with a feature flag that decouples the schema change from the application change.
Every migration must have a corresponding downgrade script. The downgrade script must be tested in staging before production deployment.
Migration executions are logged with the deploying identity, migration version, and timestamp. Migration events are written to the audit store.
DROP TABLE, DROP COLUMN, and TRUNCATE on production tables are prohibited without explicit MRB approval. Removal of data is governed by the retention policy (§11), not ad-hoc DDL.

No UPDATE, no DELETE may be applied to audit.audit_event_local, audit.audit_event_signature, engagement.engagement_status_history, workflow.workflow_transition. ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN is permitted where the new column is nullable and does not break the hash chain.
Adding enum values is permitted via ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE. Removing enum values is prohibited without a coordinated data migration and MRB approval.
Tightening a CHECK constraint requires a backfill plan and staging validation. Relaxing a constraint requires CRO review where the constraint enforces a separation-of-duties or gating rule.

The following table consolidates the primary index strategy across all schema groups. Indexes specified in §6 DDL are reproduced here for planning and review purposes. The SE shall validate index coverage against the query patterns observed in the first 30 days post-MVP.
Fuzzy firm-name matching for intake deduplication.
Role-based and status filters in admin UI.
user_id, scope_type, scope_id WHERE not revoked
Authorization checks on every API call.

The following S3 buckets are provisioned as part of the Onboarding Engine AWS infrastructure (WP-1). All buckets are encrypted with KMS. Versioning is enabled on all buckets. Object Lock (compliance mode) is enabled on audit and books-and-records buckets. Cross-Region Replication (CRR) is enabled on critical buckets as noted.
Submitted client documents — formation docs, KYC, UBO declarations, project package files. S3 URI referenced in document.uploaded_document.object_uri. Access via short-lived controlled-access tokens.
Legal package documents — engagement letters, NDAs, confidentiality agreements, corporate resolutions, authorized-representative documents. Metadata only stored in DB; files in S3.
Long-term audit event archive for compliance and external review access. Object Lock compliance mode for WORM posture.
Data export records — engagement and project exports generated for client delivery or counsel review.

Bucket names follow the convention tca-onbeng-{env}-{purpose}. Environment values: prod, staging. Document files are never read directly by Reference or Risk Engines from these buckets — Reference accesses via short-lived controlled-access tokens scoped per project_id, issued by svc:document-intake and presented at S3 access time through a presigned-URL service. There is no cross-engine bucket policy access.

All retention periods are minimum periods. Records may be retained longer where no conflicting policy applies. Deletion before the minimum retention period is prohibited without written MRB and CRO approval. The TCA retention schedule is grounded in the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 and the rules promulgated thereunder — in particular SEC Rule 204-2(a) (records to be maintained), Rule 204-2(e) (retention periods — generally 5 years), Rule 204-2(g) (WORM electronic recordkeeping), and Rule 206(4)-7 (policies and procedures).
engagement.billing_profile (tax-relevant rows), engagement.fee_model (tax-relevant rows), accounting-package metadata, invoice refs

retention_lock_until on engagement.engagement is computed as closed_at + applicable retention period from this schedule. Where a record is identified by counsel as tax-related, the 7-year period applies. The svc:audit-writer service is the single point of retention_lock application; manual updates to retention_lock are prohibited.

The three-engine architecture (Onboarding, Reference, Risk) maintains strict database ownership boundaries. No engine has direct SQL access to another engine's database schemas. All cross-engine data exchange is mediated by the documented REST API and event contracts specified in TCA-ONBENG-TRD-2026-001 §11.
Client firm, person, party-role, beneficial owner records
Reference Engine matches to external entities but does not own internal client record. Reference receives via event payloads only; no direct DB access.
Engagement, service package, project, user access grants
Reference and Risk receive via engagement.approved, project.created, user_access_grant webhook events. No direct DB access.
Reference accesses via document.submitted event + short-lived controlled-access tokens. No direct DB or S3 bucket policy access.
Source inventory, evidence, reference packs, features
Onboarding does not read or replicate Reference data. Onboarding receives reference_pack.approved, reference_status.updated, and information_request.raised event notifications only.
Onboarding does not read or replicate Risk data. Onboarding receives risk_status.updated event notifications only.
All engines write; audit service owns integrity
Onboarding writes to audit.audit_event_local (local mirror) and to the shared cross-engine store via svc:audit-writer. CRO reads across all engines via the shared store.
The following database actions are explicitly prohibited across engine boundaries:
No Onboarding Engine service identity may connect to the Reference Engine or Risk Engine Aurora cluster.
No Reference Engine or Risk Engine service identity may connect to the Onboarding Engine Aurora cluster.

No cross-engine database-level foreign keys. All cross-engine references use UUID identifiers that are validated at the API/event layer, not enforced by database constraints. (E.g., project.reference_pack_id is a UUID with no FK constraint to the Reference Engine packs schema.)
No shared database schemas. Each engine's schemas are logically and, where practical, physically isolated.
No direct S3 bucket policy access across engines. Document objects are served to Reference exclusively through short-lived controlled-access tokens scoped per project_id.
The following items remain open at DBSPEC v1.0. None block the start of build. Each carries an owner and the trigger condition for resolution.

Auto-close of Reference-originated gating exception when Reference accepts the submitted document. High-level contract specified in TRD §11.5; exact event payload and timing remains a detailed-design item for Phase 1 build.

MVP audit is captured in audit.audit_event_local and audit.audit_event_signature. Convergence with the cross-engine shared append-only audit store final schema is a detailed design item shared with the Reference and Risk DBSPECs.

Concrete row-count or volume threshold at which partitioning is introduced on audit and integration tables. Default starting trigger: 5M rows for audit, operational scan latency degradation for integration.

Selection of an external KYC/sanctions screening provider and the corresponding integration schema (e.g., KYC provider response cache in compliance schema). Currently scoped as an external API call without persisted provider response cache.

Detailed table design for QuickBooks invoice/billing sync metadata (engagement.accounting_package_status augmentation). Switchable-adapter design preserved at the application layer; database fields kept generic at MVP.
Whether document classification status is set manually by reviewers (MVP default) or augmented by an automated classifier service. Schema accommodates both today.
Concrete schemas for TCM and TCN overlay rows. Placeholders today; activated 30–60 days post-MVP go-live per TRD §26.
Selection of DocuSign vs Adobe Sign vs other for post-MVP release. Database currently stores only legal-package metadata.
The database layer for the Onboarding Engine MVP is accepted when all of the following criteria are verified by the SE and confirmed by MRB:
All ten schema groups created with correct table DDL, constraints, and indexes as specified in §6.
SE executes \d+ on each schema in the production cluster; output reviewed against this DBSPEC.
All enum types created and match §5 definitions.
SE runs \dT+ in psql; output reviewed.
Aurora cluster is Multi-AZ, encrypted with KMS, backups enabled with 35-day PITR.
AWS Console / AWS CLI verification by SE; confirmed in WP-1 infrastructure review.
Alembic migration history table is present and initial migrations are recorded.
SE runs alembic history; confirms no blank or unversioned migrations.
service_package catalog is seeded with the five MVP packages per TRD §6.1.
SE runs SELECT * FROM engagement.service_package; verifies five expected rows.
onboarding_exception_type catalog is seeded with all 15 categories per TRD §13.2.
SE runs SELECT * FROM compliance.onboarding_exception_type; verifies all categories present with default_severity and authorized_resolver_roles.
TCA overlay row exists in overlay.entity_overlay with active_flag = true.
SE runs SELECT * FROM overlay.entity_overlay WHERE entity_code = 'TCA' AND active_flag = true;
Audit tables have no UPDATE or DELETE grants to any application service identity.
SE reviews IAM role policies and database GRANT statements.
integration.integration_event polled successfully by svc:event-publisher in staging.
SE confirms delivery of a test integration event end-to-end in staging environment.
integration.inbound_event signature validation rejects an event with an invalid HMAC.
SE submits a deliberately-malformed signed payload to /api/v1/integrations/inbound-events and confirms status = SIGNATURE_INVALID and alert raised.
S3 buckets created with versioning, KMS encryption, and Object Lock compliance mode on audit-archive and legal buckets. CRR on documents, legal, audit-archive.
AWS Console / AWS CLI verification by SE.
engagement.engagement.retention_lock_until is computed on a test closure and matches the §11 schedule (5 yrs default; 7 yrs tax-related).
SE executes test closures with both retention classes and confirms computed value.

Reference-originated information_request.raised with gating_flag=true and severity=CRITICAL automatically creates a compliance.onboarding_exception with source=reference_engine and the workflow transitions to information_request_pending.
End-to-end staging test by SE; verified via audit chain replay.
VPS has zero inbound or outbound connections to the production Onboarding Engine Aurora cluster.
SE confirms no VPN, peering, or security group rules connecting VPS to Aurora.

This document may be amended by written approval of MRB and Stan Hua. Amendments are tracked as numbered revisions in the document header. Any amendment that changes physical schema DDL, index strategy, or retention policy requires SE and CRO review before the amendment is applied to the production database.
TokenCap Advisors LLC | 2678 Holdings LLC | TCA-DBSPEC-2026-ONBENG-001 v1.0 | June 2026 | CONFIDENTIAL
